result:
If I could change the world in one way, it would be to rewire the fundamental human perception of **"otherness."**  

I would make it so that every person, upon seeing or thinking of another, instantly feels a deep, visceral understanding of that person’s inner world — their fears, hopes, pains, and joys — not as an abstract idea, but as a shared reality.  

This wouldn’t erase individuality or disagreement, but it would eliminate dehumanization. Conflicts born from indifference, prejudice, greed, or hatred would have to pass through the filter of shared felt experience. Cruelty would become almost physiologically impossible. Systems of exploitation, oppression, and environmental neglect would crumble, not by decree, but because their foundations — seeing others as less real than ourselves — would dissolve.  

The change wouldn’t be in laws or technology, but in the invisible architecture of consciousness: the illusion of separateness would fall away, leaving the raw, uncomfortable, but undeniable truth of interdependence in its place.  

From that shift, everything else — justice, compassion, stewardship of the planet, creative collaboration — would follow naturally.
